# Al-Iftikhar Bugvia Foundation (AIBF)

> A Punjab Charity Commission registered Category (A) Charity (Reg: PB-6976792864708031) serving rural communities through education, healthcare, and social welfare in Bhera, Bugga Sharif, and surrounding areas of Pakistan since 2008.

## About
Al-Iftikhar Bugvia Foundation was founded by Dr. Anwaar Ahmed Bugvi in 2008. The foundation operates across Bhera (District Sargodha), Bugga Sharif, Khanna, Maliyar, Dhudian (District Jhelum), Lilla Town, and Lahore. AIBF is registered with the Punjab Charity Commission as a Category (A) Charity and is in the process of NPO/SECP Section 42 registration. Partnership with Pakistan Red Crescent Society.

## Impact (2025-2026)
- 10,000+ patients served across 5 dispensaries and 4 free health camps
- 265+ ration bags distributed in Ramadan 2025 and 2026
- 2,500+ trees planted across Bhera, Bugga, and Lahore
- 70+ women trained at 2 sewing centers (Maliyar and Bugga)
- 6 wheelchairs donated to disabled individuals
- Rs 50,000/month zakat distributed to 10 families
- Rs 135,000 autoclave donated to THQ Hospital Bhera
- 200 bags of qurbani meat to 150+ families
- Rs 100,000 flood relief to Muzaffargarh
- 2 motorbikes via Qarz Hasna for bonded labourers

## Programs (8 Active)
1. Healthcare & Medical Aid — 5 dispensaries, 4 health camps, dialysis support, hospital equipment
2. Ration & Food Security — Ramadan drives, Eid qurbani, iftaar programs, monthly flour distribution
3. Education & Skill Development — 2 sewing centers, tuition programs, school supplies, English courses
4. Livelihood & Qarz Hasna — Interest-free loans, motorbikes for delivery riders, barber shops, food carts
5. Emergency & Disaster Relief — Burn patients, fire damage, flood relief, medical emergencies
6. Zakat, Disability & Social Welfare — Monthly zakat, wheelchairs, marriage assistance, Eid packages
7. Heritage & Cultural Preservation — Zahoor Auditorium, Bhera souvenirs, postcards, Jama Masjid maintenance
8. Green Plantation Drive — Biannual tree planting, 2,500+ trees across schools, mosques, and residential areas
